mission.title: Install and Configure Ovn Kubernetes on Kubernetes
mission.description: OVN-Kubernetes is a robust networking solution for Kubernetes clusters that leverages Open Virtual Networking (OVN) and Open vSwitch (OVS). Installing OVN-Kubernetes enables advanced networking capabilities for your Kubernetes applications.

steps.title: Add the OVN-Kubernetes Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official OVN-Kubernetes Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add ovn-kubernetes https://github.com/ovn-org/ovn-kubernetes/releases/download/v1.2.0
```

steps.title: Install OVN-Kubernetes using Helm
steps.description:
Install the OVN-Kubernetes Helm chart in a new namespace called 'ovn-kubernetes' with the following command:
```bash
helm install ovn-kubernetes ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes --create-namespace --version 1.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all OVN-Kubernetes pods are running by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for OVN-Kubernetes
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, you can set resource limits for the OVN-Kubernetes components. Edit the values file or use the following command to set limits:
```bash
helm upgrade ovn-kubernetes ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OVN-Kubernetes will show all pods in the 'ovn-kubernetes' namespace as running. You can also verify the resource limits have been applied by checking the pod specifications.
codeSnippets: helm upgrade ovn-kubernetes ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OVN-Kubernetes Helm release from the cluster using the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with OVN-Kubernetes:
```bash
kubectl delete crd -l app=ovn-kubernetes
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'ovn-kubernetes' namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace ovn-kubernetes
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace ovn-kubernetes -o yaml > ovn-kubernetes-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade OVN-Kubernetes to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade ovn-kubernetes ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes --version 1.2.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that all OVN-Kubernetes pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice that OVN-Kubernetes pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs of the affected pod:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If services are not reachable, verify the service configuration and check the endpoints:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace ovn-kubernetes
kubectl get endpoints --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

troubleshooting.title: High resource usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you experience high resource usage, check the resource limits and current usage:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace ovn-kubernetes
kubectl describe deployment ovn-kubernetes --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

troubleshooting.title: Network connectivity issues
troubleshooting.description:
For network connectivity issues, check the OVN-Kubernetes network configuration and logs:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace ovn-kubernetes -o wide
kubectl logs <ovn-controller-pod-name> --namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
